name: Moroccan Brisket with Olives
recipeIngredient: 1 small Carrot, peeled, sliced into thin rounds
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the brisket with salt and pepper; rub each piece with garlic.
2. In a heavy roasting pan, sear the meat on all sides in a bit of olive oil. Remove and set aside.
3. In the same pot, add the remaining olive oil, turmeric (or saffron), ginger, and onions. Sauté until the onions are limp.
4. Add celery and carrots. Sauté for a few more minutes.
5. Add tomatoes and mix everything together.
6. Remove one-third of the mixture and place the seared meat on top of the remaining mixture.
7. Cover with the rest of the mixture and bake in a preheated oven at 350°F until the meat is tender, about 3 hours.
8. Remove from the refrigerator and let cool slightly. Pit the olives and place them in a pot. Cover with water, bring to a boil, then drain and repeat the process to remove excess saltiness.
9. Remove any fat that has collected on the brisket and slice it against the grain.
10. Return the meat to a heavy pot with the remaining mixture. Sprinkle the olives over the meat.
11. Reheat at 350°F for 30 minutes, then serve.
